title: Properties that only wrap a backing field should be auto-implemented
messageFormat: Convert this property to an auto-implemented property.
summary: When a property's getter only returns a private field and its setter only assigns that same field, the explicit backing field and accessor bodies repeat what an auto-implemented property expresses in one line. The compiler generates the same field behind the scenes.
Replacing the pattern with { get; set; } removes boilerplate and keeps the field private to the property, where it belongs.
